title: Great Himalayan National Park
tagline: Great Himalayan National Park — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Great Himalayan National Park, Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Great Himalayan National Park (GHNP) is a national park in India, located in Banjar sub-division of Kullu in the state of Himachal Pradesh.
overview.description: The Great Himalayan National Park (GHNP) is a national park in India, located in Banjar sub-division of Kullu in the state of Himachal Pradesh. The park was established in 1984 and is spread over an area of 1171 km2; elevations within the park range between 1500 and 6000 m. The Great Himalayan National Park is a habitat to numerous flora and more than 375 fauna species, including approximately 31 mammals, 181 birds, 3 reptiles, 9 amphibians, 11 annelids, 17 mollusks and 127 insects. They are protected under the strict guidelines of the Wildlife Protection Act of 1972; hence any sort of hunting

topPlaces.name: Bhaba Pass
topPlaces.description: Bhaba Pass, also known as Bhawa Pass, is a high mountain pass (elevation 4,890 metres (16,040 ft)) situated in the northern Indian state of Himachal Pradesh in the Indian Himalayas. It is located on the border of Kinnaur district and Lahaul and Spiti District, and borders Pin Valley National Park and the Rupi-Bhaba Wildlife Sanctuary.

topPlaces.name: Rupi Bhaba Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Rupi Bhaba Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in Kinnaur district, Himachal Pradesh, India. It was established in 1982 and covers an area of about 503 km2 in the upper catchment of the Bhaba Valley.

items.note: Bhaba Pass, also known as Bhawa Pass, is a high mountain pass (elevation 4,890 metres (16,040 ft)) situated in the northern Indian state of Himachal Pradesh in the Indian Himalayas. It is located on the border of Kinnaur district and Lahaul and Spiti District, and borders Pin Valley National Park and the Rupi-Bhaba Wildlife Sanctuary.

Rupi Bhaba Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area located in Kinnaur district, Himachal Pradesh, India. It was established in 1982 and covers an area of about 503 km2 in the upper catchment of the Bhaba Valley.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Great Himalayan National Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Great Himalayan National Park is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Great Himalayan National Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (165 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Great Himalayan National Park?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Great Himalayan National Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Great Himalayan National Park?
faq.a: Stays in Great Himalayan National Park start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Great Himalayan National Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bhaba Pass, Rupi Bhaba Wildlife Sanctuary.
